title: CORE: Uniform exponential integrability sup_N E_μ[F_N^{1+δ}] < ∞ (unbounded cubic in the exponent)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Step D — the crux.** Let $F_N=\frac{dT_\psi^*\mu_N}{d\mu_N}=e^{X_N}$ with $X_N=\langle\psi,u\rangle_H^\sim-\tfrac12\|\psi\|_H^2-[V_N(u)-V_N(u-\psi)]$. To conclude $T_\psi^*\mu\ll\mu$ with $\mathbb{E}_\mu F_\psi=1$, one needs $F_N\to F_\psi$ in $L^1(\mu)$, for which it suffices to show $\sup_N\mathbb{E}_\mu[F_N^{1+\delta}]<\infty$ for some $\delta>0$.

**Gap:** The exponent $X_N$ contains $-\int\psi\,{:}u^3{:}$, a cubic that is **unbounded below**, so direct exponential-moment bounds diverge. Provide a genuine argument, e.g.:
- the **Boué–Dupuis variational representation** of $\log\mathbb{E}_\mu e^{(1+\delta)X_N}$ as a stochastic control problem, with the cubic term absorbed by the drift energy using $\|\psi\|_{C^k}<\infty$; or
- a Nelson/hypercontractivity argument adapted to $\Phi^4_3$.

**Why very hard:** this is research-level. It is the single estimate that makes absolute continuity work; everything else is bookkeeping by comparison. Reference: Barashkov–Gubinelli, *A variational method for $\Phi^4_3$*, Duke 2020.
